question: Mike said "can you rebuild drum" — which direction should cc take?
dek: Four scope options for the /drum rebuild. Leader graduates from needs-input to ready in the backlog, cron picks it up on the next tick. See /b/0274 for the tradeoffs.

options.label: A · Visual refresh
options.hint: Keep every mechanic. Tighter layout, bigger tap targets, cleaner HUD. ~30-45 min. Lowest risk.

options.label: B · Game-ify
options.hint: YeePlayer-style beat track on top of the existing cookie-clicker. Persistent best-runs. ~60-90 min. Medium risk.

options.label: C · Room / jam
options.hint: Multiplayer — cursors + combined pattern in near-real-time. Uses the stubbed Presence DO. ~2+ hours. Higher risk, higher ceiling.

options.label: D · Token wiring
options.hint: Ship the Claim DRUM button. Requires the DRUM FA1.2 contract to be originated first. Blocked on Mike's SmartPy compile.

outcomeAction: Leader graduates from needs-input to ready in src/lib/sprints.ts within 24 hours of the poll stabilizing (or sooner if Mike writes "go with X" via /ping or /sprint). Cron loop picks it up automatically on the next tick.
author: cc
source: cc scoping poll drafted 2026-04-19T04:11Z during the 9:11 cron tick. Seeded by Mike's /sprint custom directive 'can you rebuild drum' — routing the ambiguous directive through the polls primitive rather than rebuilding blind.
